flow_name: Real QDM — Reverse Engineering from SQL
cover_narration: In this walkthrough, we'll reverse engineer an existing SQL schema into Quest Data Modeler... turning a script file into a complete data model in just a few clicks. Let's get started.
outro_narration: And just like that... a SQL script becomes a full data model with entities, attributes, and relationships ready to evolve. Thanks for watching.

steps.label: Wait for the Overview page
steps.narration: We start on the Overview page...

steps.label: Locate the Reverse Engineering Quick Action
steps.narration: And spot the Reverse Engineering action card.

steps.label: Open the Reverse Engineering wizard
steps.narration: Click it to open the wizard.

steps.label: Wait for the wizard to render
steps.narration: The wizard asks how we want to reverse engineer.

steps.label: Switch to single-page form
steps.narration: Switch to the single-page view to see every option at once.

steps.label: Choose Script file as the source
steps.narration: We'll pick Script file... uploading a SQL script lets the modeler build the model offline.

steps.label: Wait for the upload field to appear
Upload the sample retail schema
steps.narration: Upload our sample retail schema. It defines four tables: Customer, Product, Orders, and OrderLine, with foreign keys tying them together.

steps.label: Blur the Version dropdown to commit
Click outside to ensure form commits
Expand the Parse Results section
steps.narration: Open the Parse Results section to analyze the script.

steps.label: Modeler parses the SQL
steps.narration: The modeler parses the SQL script. It extracts every table, column, primary key, and foreign key automatically.

steps.label: Click OK to import
steps.narration: Click OK to bring everything into a brand new model.

steps.label: Wait for the editor to load with the imported entities
steps.narration: Give it a moment as the modeling canvas loads...

steps.label: Final state — entities visible on the canvas
steps.narration: And there it is. Customer, Product, Orders, and OrderLine, plus the relationships, ready to extend or evolve.
